skill.name: doc-sync-tool
skill.description: Automatically synchronize Agents.md, claude.md, and gemini.md files in your project to maintain consistent AI agent documentation across multiple platforms.
skill.summary: A file synchronization tool that keeps AI agent configuration documents consistent by automatically discovering, syncing, and watching for changes across AGENTS.md, CLAUDE.md, and GEMINI.md files.
skill.author: ZhanlinCui

security_audit.summary: Static analysis flagged 254 external_commands patterns and 31 filesystem operations, but evaluation confirms these are false positives. The 'Ruby/shell backtick execution' findings are markdown documentation examples in EXAMPLES.md, INDEX.md, README.md, QUICKSTART.md, and SKILL.md - not executable code. The 'hex-encoded characters' and 'weak cryptographic algorithm' findings are ANSI terminal color codes (e.g., \x1b[32m for green). The tool performs legitimate file synchronization using Node.js fs module with proper path handling via path.join() and path.dirname(). One minor concern: rename-quick.js uses child_process.execSync with hardcoded 'find' commands, which is acceptable but should be noted.

low_findings.title: Shell Command Execution in Helper Script
low_findings.description: The rename-quick.js helper script uses child_process.execSync to run 'find' commands for file discovery. While the arguments are hardcoded and not user-controllable, this represents a minor security consideration for a helper utility.

content.value_statement: Maintaining identical configuration files for multiple AI agents is tedious and error-prone. This tool automatically keeps AGENTS.md, CLAUDE.md, and GEMINI.md files in sync across your entire project with real-time file watching or manual triggering.
actual_capabilities: Recursively scans project directories to discover AGENTS.md, CLAUDE.md, and GEMINI.md files
Automatically creates missing documentation files by copying content from the most recently modified source
Real-time file watching with debouncing to detect and sync changes instantly
Excludes common directories like node_modules, .git, .next, dist, and .obsidian
Supports both manual sync execution and continuous background monitoring via PM2
Provides colored terminal output with detailed sync status and statistics
limitations: Requires Node.js 14 or higher runtime environment
File watching may have limited support on some network filesystems
Does not handle merge conflicts - always overwrites with the newest file content
Cannot sync files across different directories, only within the same folder

use_cases.title: New Project Setup
use_cases.description: When starting a new project, create just one agent config file (e.g., CLAUDE.md) and let the tool automatically generate AGENTS.md and GEMINI.md with identical content.
use_cases.target_user: Developers starting projects with multi-agent support

use_cases.title: Continuous Documentation Sync
use_cases.description: Run the tool in watch mode during development to automatically propagate any agent configuration changes to all other agent files in real-time.
use_cases.target_user: Teams maintaining documentation for Claude, Gemini, and other AI assistants

use_cases.title: Bulk Synchronization
use_cases.description: Execute manual sync across multiple project directories at once to ensure all agent documentation stays consistent after major updates.
use_cases.target_user: Project managers coordinating documentation across repositories

prompt_templates.title: Basic Setup
prompt_templates.prompt: Set up automatic documentation sync for my project. I have a CLAUDE.md file and need to create matching AGENTS.md and GEMINI.md files.
prompt_templates.scenario: First-time user wants to initialize sync

prompt_templates.title: Manual Sync Execution
prompt_templates.prompt: Run the doc-sync-tool to synchronize all agent documentation files in the current project directory and show me which files were updated.
prompt_templates.scenario: User made changes and wants to sync manually

prompt_templates.title: Background Monitoring
prompt_templates.prompt: Start the file watcher in background mode using PM2 so my agent docs stay synchronized automatically. Show me how to verify it is running.

prompt_templates.title: Configuration Customization
prompt_templates.prompt: Help me modify the doc-sync-tool configuration to exclude additional directories and change the target file names to match my project conventions.

output_examples.input: Run manual sync on a project with one existing CLAUDE.md file
output: ✓ 找到 1 个目录包含目标文件
✅ 同步完成！处理目录：1 个，更新文件：2 个，耗时：0.23s

output_examples.input: File watcher detects a change to CLAUDE.md

best_practices: Run the tool in watch mode during active development to keep all agent docs synchronized in real-time
Use PM2 for production environments to ensure the file watcher survives system restarts
Commit all three documentation files to version control for complete backup and history tracking
anti_patterns: Do not manually edit multiple agent files simultaneously - let the sync tool handle propagation
Avoid running multiple sync instances on the same directory to prevent race conditions
Do not exclude directories that actually contain agent documentation you want synchronized

faq.question: What files does this tool synchronize?
faq.answer: The tool synchronizes three files: AGENTS.md (or Agents.md), CLAUDE.md (or claude.md), and GEMINI.md (or gemini.md). It matches files case-insensitively within the same directory.

faq.question: How does the tool decide which file is the source?
faq.answer: The tool uses modification time (mtime) to determine the source. The most recently modified file becomes the source, and its content is copied to the other files.

faq.question: Can I customize which files are synchronized?
faq.answer: Yes. Edit the targetFiles array in sync.js to change which filenames are synchronized. The default is ['AGENTS.md', 'CLAUDE.md', 'GEMINI.md'].

faq.question: Does the watch mode consume a lot of system resources?
faq.answer: No. The watch mode uses Node.js native fs.watch with recursive monitoring. It consumes less than 50MB of memory and near-zero CPU when idle.

faq.question: How do I stop the file watcher?
faq.answer: Press Ctrl+C in the terminal to stop. If running via PM2, use 'pm2 stop doc-sync' or 'pm2 delete doc-sync' to stop and remove the service.

faq.question: What happens if two files are modified at the same time?
faq.answer: The tool includes debouncing to handle rapid changes. It will use whichever file has the most recent modification timestamp after the debounce period completes.
